title: 2025 Formula One Championship
subtitle: A scroll-driven map of the Norris, Verstappen and Piastri title fight.
dataSources: Formula1.com race reports for key-race annotation

races.label: Australia
races.summary: Rain, safety cars and tyre changes made the opener a stress test; Norris left with the first real championship gap.
races.chapterTitle: Rain made the opener a control test

races.label: Qatar
races.summary: A lap-7 Safety Car offered every team a cheap pit stop. Only Verstappen took it, and that single call reshaped the standings and pushed the title fight to the final round.
races.chapterTitle: The safety car McLaren did not convert

races.label: United Arab Emirates
races.summary: Qatar leaves the title unresolved; Abu Dhabi resolves the three-way fight with Norris doing exactly enough while Verstappen wins.

summaryBands.title: Opening shape
summaryBands.text: McLaren starts the year as the constant reference point, while Verstappen keeps enough pressure in the system to make every dropped point visible.

summaryBands.title: Summer compression
summaryBands.text: The title fight narrows into a three-driver problem: clean execution, tyre windows and intra-team pressure matter more than raw peaks.

summaryBands.title: Late volatility
summaryBands.text: The final third turns on retirements, penalties, disqualifications and strategy decisions rather than simple race pace.
